title: Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual
caption:
Share of people who identify as religious or spiritual, 2023

Across 24 countries in 2023, the composition of religious and spiritual identity varies significantly, with stacked bars showing the distribution of five response categories: "Religious," "Both religious and spiritual," "Spiritual," "Neither religious nor spiritual," and "Did not answer/'I don't know'." India shows the highest share identifying as religious, with the dark blue segment extending past 60%, followed by Nigeria and Turkey, where the religious category also dominates the left portion of the bar. In contrast, Japan, Australia, and Sweden display minimal religious identification, with the dark blue segment under 10% in each. The "Both religious and spiritual" category, shown in light green, is most prominent in Tanzania, the Philippines, and Kenya, where it occupies a substantial middle section of the bar. The "Spiritual" category, in dark green, is largest in the United States, Argentina, and Israel, often appearing as the second or third largest segment. The "Neither religious nor spiritual" response, in pink, dominates the right side of bars for Spain, the United Kingdom, Germany, and Hong Kong, frequently exceeding 40% of the total. Mexico and South Africa show high shares in the "Spiritual" category, while Poland and Egypt have relatively small pink segments. The gray "Did not answer" portion remains consistently small across all countries, rarely exceeding 5%. The data excludes international aviation and is based on survey responses from the Global Flourishing Study (2024). The most visible pattern is the inverse relationship between the size of the religious segment and the neither religious nor spiritual segment, with countries at opposite ends of this spectrum showing little overlap in these two categories.
